title: Ticket Aging by Priority
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" state IN ("new","in_progress","on_hold")
| eval age_days=round((now()-opened_at)/86400,1)
| eval bucket=case(age_days<=1,"0-1d", age_days<=7,"2-7d", age_days<=30,"8-30d", true(),"30d+")
| eval pri=case(priority=1,"P1", priority=2,"P2", priority=3,"P3", priority=4,"P4", true(),"Other")
| stats count by pri, bucket
| sort pri, bucket
description: Distribution of open ticket age by priority highlights backlog imbalance (e.g., many old P3s) and supports capacity and escalation decisions.
value: Distribution of open ticket age by priority highlights backlog imbalance (e.g., many old P3s) and supports capacity and escalation decisions.
implementation: Normalize priority labels. Bucket age for open tickets only. Report stacked bar or pivot table (priority × age bucket). Alert when count in `30d+` for P1/P2 exceeds policy.
visualization: Stacked bar (age buckets by priority), Heatmap (priority × bucket), Table (raw counts).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Open incidents (`sourcetype=snow:incident`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize priority labels. Bucket age for open tickets only. Report stacked bar or pivot table (priority × age bucket). Alert when count in `30d+` for P1/P2 exceeds policy.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" state IN ("new","in_progress","on_hold")
| eval age_days=round((now()-opened_at)/86400,1)
| eval bucket=case(age_days<=1,"0-1d", age_days<=7,"2-7d", age_days<=30,"8-30d", true(),"30d+")
| eval pri=case(priority=1,"P1", priority=2,"P2", priority=3,"P3", priority=4,"P4", true(),"Other")
| stats count by pri, bucket
| sort pri, bucket
```

Understanding this SPL

**Ticket Aging by Priority** — Distribution of open ticket age by priority highlights backlog imbalance (e.g., many old P3s) and supports capacity and escalation decisions.

Documented **Data sources**: Open incidents (`sourcetype=snow:incident`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **bucket** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pri** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pri, bucket** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (age buckets by priority), Heatmap (priority × bucket), Table (raw counts).
grandmaExplanation: Distribution of open ticket age by priority highlights backlog imbalance (many old P3s) and supports capacity and escalation decisions — so tickets, changes, and incidents are on track and nothing is silently missed.
